findings.reachability: Any unauthenticated or authenticated HTTP client that can send a POST request to an endpoint using this middleware; no special privileges required
findings.justification: Line 75 sets `req.method = method.toUpperCase()` to an attacker-supplied value (from query string or header) after only a whitelist check against known HTTP method names; if authorization middleware runs downstream and makes decisions based on `req.method`, an attacker can escalate a POST (allowed) to DELETE/PUT/PATCH to bypass route-level access controls.

detection_pattern.description: Catches middleware that overwrites req.method with caller-controlled data; attacker can masquerade as a privileged HTTP verb if auth middleware runs after this assignment.

findings.location: supports(), line 143; methods npm package whitelist
findings.reachability: Any client that can send a POST request with `_method=TRACE` (or `X-HTTP-Method-Override: TRACE`) to a server that reflects or echoes TRACE requests
findings.justification: The `methods` npm package (line 17, used in `supports()` at line 143) includes `trace` and `connect`; a POST to `/?_method=TRACE` sets `req.method='TRACE'`, enabling Cross-Site Tracing (XST) if the server or any upstream proxy reflects TRACE content including cookies or auth headers.

detection_pattern.description: Catches method-override patterns that allow TRACE/CONNECT through the whitelist; servers that reflect TRACE are vulnerable to Cross-Site Tracing which can exfiltrate cookies.

findings.reachability: Exploitable only when developer sets `opts.methods = null`; a GET or HEAD request with an `_method` override param would then be eligible to trigger method substitution, bypassing CSRF defenses that rely on POST-only semantics
findings.justification: Line 53 assigns `opts.methods` directly when it is not `undefined`, so passing `null` sets `methods = null`; line 64's guard `if (methods && methods.indexOf(...) === -1)` short-circuits on `null` (falsy), skipping the allowed-method check entirely and letting ANY HTTP verb (GET, HEAD) trigger an override — removing the POST-only CSRF barrier.

detection_pattern.section_name: CSRF bypass: null methods config disables verb guard
detection_pattern.description: Catches method-override configurations that accept null to mean 'all methods allowed', which removes the POST-only guard and lets GET/HEAD requests override HTTP methods, undermining CSRF protections.
